rationale: The citing paper uses Kivetz and Simonson (2002a) as a key prior result for the claim that choosing hedonic over functional options evokes guilt, then explicitly positions its own contribution as going beyond that finding. It repeatedly contrasts its framework with the earlier work, stating that prior research focused on a narrower part of the functional-versus-hedonic trade-off space and that this paper expands to additional negative and positive emotions and mediation tests. In the general discussion, it again treats the cited paper as the immediate antecedent and explains how its experiments broaden and qualify that earlier account.
relation_description: The 2007 paper builds directly on Kivetz and Simonson (2002a) by extending the guilt-based hedonic-versus-functional trade-off line to a richer set of specific emotions and to downstream preference patterns (including choice vs. WTP differences). The cited paper is used as the baseline prior finding that the new model and experiments expand.
